2606.17973.title: Fine-tuning LLMs for Passive Depression Severity Estimation from AI Mental Health Dialogue
2606.17973.paper_summary: Depression is the leading cause of disability worldwide, and early detection of symptom change is essential for timely intervention. Validated instruments such as the Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9) support symptom monitoring at scale, but real-world completion rates are low, introducing response bias and systematic missingness. Passive approaches that infer severity from routinely generated data could close this gap. We address this by predicting PHQ-9 total scores directly from transcripts of conversations between users and an AI mental health application, requiring only conversation text and no additional clinical data. We fine-tune a Qwen3.5-27B backbone with a regression head, augment 3,111 ground-truth labels with pseudolabels generated by a reasoning model (Claude Opus) and iteratively trained intermediate models, for a combined dataset of 6,283 users. On a held-out test set of 842 users, our best model achieves MAE = 2.6, RMSE = 4.0, Pearson r = 0.80, and AUC = 0.91 at the PHQ-9 >= 10 clinical threshold. We also find AUC > 0.87 at every severity threshold from PHQ-9 >= 3 to PHQ-9 >= 24, demonstrating that the model captures depression severity across the full clinical spectrum. This work opens the door to passive, continuous symptom monitoring in AI mental health platforms, without requiring users to complete self-report measures.
2606.17973.paper_summary_zh: 抑鬱症是全球主要的殘疾原因，及早發現症狀變化對於及時干預至關重要。經過驗證的工具，如患者健康問卷-9（PHQ-9），支持大規模的症狀監測，但現實世界中的完成率較低，導致反應偏差和系統性缺失。從常規生成的數據中推斷嚴重程度的被動方法可以彌補這一差距。我們通過直接從用戶與AI心理健康應用程序之間的對話記錄中預測PHQ-9總分來解決這個問題，只需對話文本而不需要額外的臨床數據。我們對Qwen3.5-27B骨幹進行微調，並添加回歸頭，將3,111個真實標籤與由推理模型（Claude Opus）生成的偽標籤進行增強，並對中間模型進行迭代訓練，形成一個包含6,283名用戶的綜合數據集。在842名用戶的保留測試集中，我們的最佳模型達到MAE = 2.6，RMSE = 4.0，Pearson r = 0.80，以及在PHQ-9 >= 10臨床閾值下的AUC = 0.91。我們還發現在PHQ-9 >= 3到PHQ-9 >= 24的每個嚴重程度閾值下，AUC均大於0.87，證明該模型能夠捕捉到整個臨床範圍內的抑鬱症嚴重程度。這項工作為AI心理健康平台中的被動、持續症狀監測打開了大門，而無需用戶完成自我報告措施。
2606.17973.author: Olivier Tieleman et.al.
2606.17973.authors: Olivier Tieleman, Ziyi Zhu, Ting Su, Samuel J. Bell, Thomas D. Hull, Caitlin A. Stamatis
